conditions.critical_condition_basis: Chromatographic critical point where enthalpic and entropic terms compensate, resulting in loss of separation selectivity according to molecular weight
conditions.evidence_text: At the composition of 57/43 (v/v) CH2Cl2 /CH3CN, all the PS standards are eluted as a single peak. This is the chromatographic critical point or the adsorption-size exclusion transition point where the enthalpic and entropic terms in Eq. (2) compensate each other. The critical condition is observed at 30.5 8 C, consistent with the result shown in Fig. 2.
conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry and Polymer Research Institute, POSTECH, Pohang, 790-784 KOREA
conditions.notes: The text explicitly identifies this setup as the 'chromatographic critical point' for Polystyrene.
